accepted.claim: CoreWeave has approximately two dozen named customers.

raw_pool.claim: OpenAI inked a five-year deal with CoreWeave for $11.9 billion in capacity in March 2025, with an additional $10.5 billion added later, bringing the total to $22.4 billion.
OpenAI represents approximately 25.5 percent of CoreWeave's extended revenue backlog.
Meta Platforms and OpenAI together represent nearly two-thirds of CoreWeave's guaranteed revenue backlog.
In 2024, Microsoft represented 62 percent of CoreWeave's $1.92 billion in sales.
CoreWeave's total revenue in 2024 was $1.92 billion.
CoreWeave's top three customers accounted for 77 percent of revenues in 2024.
CoreWeave had $5.13 billion in sales in 2025, up 2.7X from 2024.
CoreWeave had an operating loss of $46 million in 2025.
CoreWeave posted a net loss of $1.17 billion in 2025 due to stock compensation and other factors.
CoreWeave has just shy of $4 billion in cash and marketable securities.
CoreWeave spent $14.9 billion on capital equipment expenses in 2025, building out its datacenter fleet to 43 centers.
CoreWeave ended 2025 with 850 megawatts of active power across its datacenter fleet.
CoreWeave is estimated to have approximately 600,000 GPUs, mostly Nvidia H100s and H200s with a rising number of B200s and B300s.
CoreWeave has 3.5 gigawatts of total power under contract.
CoreWeave's GPU fleet is estimated to be worth between $15 billion and $20 billion before significant depreciation.
CoreWeave has 2.25 gigawatts of unused contracted power to potentially build out.
At Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang's cited figure of $50 billion per gigawatt, CoreWeave would need approximately $113 billion to build out its unused contracted power capacity.
Nvidia's cited figure for datacenter buildout cost is approximately $50 billion per gigawatt of capacity.
CoreWeave announced a private offering of $1.75 billion in senior notes due in 2031.
CoreWeave announced a $3 billion offering of senior convertible notes due in 2031.
CoreWeave planned capital expenditures of $6–7 billion for early 2026, with full-year 2026 investment expected between $30–35 billion.
CoreWeave went public in March 2025.
CoreWeave had approximately 43 datacenters at end of 2025.
Neocloud rental pricing is estimated at roughly half the rate of major hyperscalers, which charge 4X–5X the buildout cost over time.
CoreWeave's revenue grew 2.7X year-over-year from 2024 to 2025.
Elon Musk is floating a 100 times revenue multiple valuation for Anthropic, implying a $3 trillion IPO valuation.
Approximately 40% of data center builds are reported to be behind schedule.
Hyperscaler CapEx spend is described as being in the hundreds of billions of dollars.
Oracle beat earnings estimates for Q3 FY26, causing Wall Street to send the stock upward.
